title: putoria calabrica var. hispanica
intro: Putoria calabrica var. hispanica is a lesser-known plant species within the putoria calabrica plant, also known as the wood sorrel or red wood sorrel, which is a type of flowering plant in the oxalis family. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Putoria calabrica var. hispanica include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and wound healer. Herbal preparations of Putoria calabrica var. hispanica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
